  • ⚠️ BREAKING APIs related to the Privacy Sandbox have been modified. The public enum PrivacySandboxService::PromptSuppressedReason has been removed. Additionally, the custom_key_commitment and custom_issuer fields have been removed from the public Mojo interface network::mojom::TrustTokenParams. (41e4d73) (e4f0243)

APIs & Extensions

  • The chrome.debugger API has been updated to expose the WebMCP domain, allowing developers to experiment with the feature directly from extensions. (71a14cd)
  • The TabStrip API was enhanced to support context menus, and the Glic component extension now supports glic.invoke. (b796e06) (d1bdfa4)
  • Several deprecated or unused extension APIs have been removed, including the webrtcAudioPrivate API and the chrome.appviewTag API on desktop platforms. (6f72765) (a7c9dd8)
  • A bug was fixed where the userVisibleOnly: false setting for silent push notifications was not persisted across browser restarts. The setting is now correctly saved in user preferences. (dd5be66)
  • Internal PWA logic was refactored to better decouple installation and launch logic by exposing more app control to the WebContentsManager. (062e13d)

Networking & Security

  • A new net::CryptoPrivateKey class has been introduced to facilitate migrating away from BoringSSL-specific APIs for private key operations, improving cross-platform compatibility. (d0a78a5)
  • A security fix prevents a potential leak of device attributes to regular HTTPS origins that are listed in an Isolated Web App's scope_extensions. (8e46d53)
  • A bug in the Happy Eyeballs v3 implementation that was breaking TLS 1.2 renegotiation for client certificate authentication has been fixed. (51bda44)
  • The enterprise policy PrivacySandboxPromptEnabled has been deprecated and its enforcement logic removed, as the associated user prompt is being removed from the browser. (ff025fc)
  • A bug was fixed to prevent sensitive IBANs from being stored in the autocomplete history table. (245dd79)

Browser UI & Features

  • The refreshed Infobar UI and its prioritization logic have been enabled by default. (e6f0526)
  • The ToolbarButtonProvider has been updated to support UnownedUserData, allowing clients to depend on it without a direct dependency on BrowserView or Browser, improving component decoupling. (d37c85a)
  • A performance issue in TranslatePrefs that caused unnecessary disk writes on every navigation was fixed by adding an early-out and optimizing preference updates. (b27227f)
  • A use-after-free crash in the TabStripActionContainer was resolved by using a WeakPtr for an animation callback. (213ddd4)

Autofill & Passwords

  • The Credential Management API (navigator.credentials.get()) has been updated to correctly respect the user's biometric re-authentication setting. (6b43fb9)
  • The internal PasswordStore implementation has been refactored to use the modern StoredCredential struct instead of the legacy PasswordForm, as part of an ongoing modernization effort. (1b95806)
  • A crash was fixed that could occur when merging Autofill profiles that contained phone numbers with invalid country codes. (333c435)

Core Internals & Testing

  • A significant modularization effort was completed for the //chrome/browser/device_reauth component, moving it out of the monolithic //chrome/browser target to improve dependency boundaries and build times. (dab3955)
  • A widespread refactoring effort replaced repeated parsing of the New Tab Page URL with cached GURL objects across many components to improve performance. (ec0f7b5) (9e92c17) (9abdf92) (910f0e9)
  • Support for Google FuzzTest has been enabled for Windows builds, expanding the availability of this modern fuzzing engine. (be6f9db)
  • Several obsolete classes from the legacy audio input path have been removed, including AudioInputStreamHandle, MojoAudioInputStream, and AudioInputDelegate. (a3de08e)

2026-04-19

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-19

Overview

This digest covers 28 commits, of which 10 were analyzed after filtering for changes relevant to Windows/macOS embedders, focusing on browser APIs, UI, networking, and security. The analysis spans from commit (174be1f) to (6fb6cb0). No major breaking changes to stable public APIs were identified in this set.

Browser UI

  • The implementation of tab hover cards has been refactored to use ui::views::BubbleAnchor, an internal change to simplify UI code. (174be1f)
  • Minor UI adjustments were made to the Profile Picker and First Run Experience (FRE) screens, including reordering buttons for better user flow and temporarily disabling animations on the sign-in page. (e73f207) (88eebcc)

Networking

  • The SQL-based network disk cache (net/disk_cache/sql) received several internal updates to improve asynchronous task tracking, primarily for testing. New helper classes like SqlAsyncTaskManager and SqlTrackedSequenceBound were introduced to enable more reliable test synchronization without affecting runtime behavior. (0e4d1c9) (d809804) (38c4f09)

Autofill & Payments

  • New metrics were added to log user interactions with credit cards that were saved via other Google services or have not yet been used in Chrome. This will provide insights for the Autofill Downstream in-product help (IPH) feature. (6fb6cb0)
  • The text in the in-product help (IPH) bubble for saved cards has been updated to mention "Google Wallet" instead of "Google Account" to align with branding efforts. (7ba1c54)

Infrastructure

  • Two expired histograms related to the browser's process singleton startup logic on Windows (Chrome.ProcessSingleton.TerminateProcessErrorCode.Windows and Chrome.ProcessSingleton.TimeToFailure) have been removed from the codebase. (3db3a99) (2cbe2ff)

2026-04-18

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-18

Overview

This digest covers 48 commits from the Chromium repository, with a focus on changes relevant to Windows and macOS embedders. Key updates include breaking changes in the Remoting and URL components, significant refactoring of Windows security object handling, and various improvements to UI, extensions, and accessibility. Commits span from (9bfcec8)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((9bfcec8)) to (83cace3)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((83cace3)).

Remoting

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The legacy ICE-based connection classes IceConnectionToClient and IceConnectionToHost have been removed. ChromotingHost now exclusively uses WebrtcConnectionToClient. This is a significant step in the removal of the legacy Chromotocol. (14c775e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Following the removal of the ICE connection classes, the underlying legacy transport classes IceTransport and IceTransportChannel, along with their dependencies like ChannelMultiplexer, have also been deleted. (e8ee844)

Networking & URL

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The deprecated, unsafe url::IsStandard(const char* spec, const Component& scheme) overload has been removed from url/url_util.h. Callers must migrate to the IsStandard(std::optional<std::string_view> scheme) overload. (83cace3)
  • WebSocket handshakes over HTTP/3 will now correctly populate QUIC version information and delegate to the session handle for QUIC-specific error details, aligning its behavior with QuicHttpStream. (e7db959)

Base & System (Windows)

  • A significant refactoring has removed dependencies on ATL security objects (like CSecurityDesc, CDacl, CSid) from chrome/updater and chrome/enterprise_companion. These have been replaced with their modern base::win equivalents (base::win::SecurityDescriptor, base::win::Sid). As part of this, base::win::SecurityDescriptor gained a new SetDaclEntry overload that accepts a base::win::AccessToken. (c7f06b9)

UI & Accessibility

  • A bug on Windows has been fixed where the context menu item to pin/unpin the tab search button to the toolbar was not functional. The command is now correctly handled. (95f3647)
  • Accessibility on macOS for spin buttons (<input type="number">) has been improved. The browser now sends specific increment/decrement event intents, preventing VoiceOver from misinterpreting value changes as text edits and ensuring it announces the full new value. (9bfcec8)

Extensions

  • A new GlicPrivate.invoke endpoint has been implemented to allow fetching prompts by prompt_id. This is part of the ongoing development for the Glic feature. (0845cc7)

Internal Refactoring & Cleanup

  • Unused PWA manifest update utilities (manifest_update_utils.h/.cc) and an associated UMA histogram have been removed, cleaning up code orphaned by the migration to a modern job-based architecture for manifest updates. (9338d92)
  • As part of a large-scale refactor, consumers of OptimizationGuideModelProvider within the segmentation platform are being migrated to use the OptimizationGuideGlobalState API. This consolidates the model provider into a globally managed asset. (63b7b2d)

2026-04-17

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-17

Overview

This summary covers 546 relevant commits out of 763 total for the main branch on April 17th, 2026. The changes span from the first commit ((d5044d6)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((d5044d6))) to the last ((9a36092)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((9a36092))), with a strong focus on breaking API changes, security hardening, and updates to the extensions platform.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The entire synchronous OSCrypt backend has been removed. The components/os_crypt/sync directory, including the OSCryptMocker for testing, is deleted. Embedders must migrate to the asynchronous os_crypt_async APIs. On Windows, calls to OSCrypt::Init must be replaced with os_crypt_async::Init. (a4cab4a) (21b80f4) (51493b3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method ToolbarButtonProvider::GetAvatarToolbarButton() has been removed. Embedders must migrate to the new GetAvatarToolbarButtonInterface() method to access the avatar button view. (6c9ccc1)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The deprecated public method net::HashValue::DeprecatedFromString has been removed. Code must be updated to use the net::HashValue::FromString static factory method, which returns an std::optional. (104e45e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The SharedImageInfo and SharedImageMetadata structs have been moved from gpu/command_buffer/client/ to gpu/command_buffer/common/shared_image_info.h. Embedders using these GPU client interfaces must update their include paths. (59eee09)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The virtual method CreateChromeMetadataPacketRecorder has been reverted and removed from the public content::TracingDelegate interface. Embedders implementing this delegate will fail to compile. (956f380)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature for PrePrefetchService::Create in content/public/browser/pre_prefetch_service.h has been changed. Two bool parameters are now std::optional<bool>, which is a source- and binary-incompatible change. (7a24f21)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The method signature for OnMediaMetadataChanged in content/browser/media/media_web_contents_observer.h has changed, swapping the order of has_audio and has_video parameters. Downstream overrides will break. (1a7e135)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The passage_embeddings::Embedding component API no longer has a public Dimensions() method. Clients must update their code to use GetData().size() instead. (49a6256)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The blink::mojom::DevicePostureProvider mojo interface has been changed. OverrideDevicePostureForEmulation and DisableDevicePostureOverrideForEmulation were removed, breaking consumers in testing and emulation scenarios. (829bf4e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Extensions using Declarative Net Request (DNR) to redirect to or from file:// URLs will now fail unless the extension has been granted the "Allow access to file URLs" permission. This may break existing extension functionality. (53caa53)

Security

  • The extensions EventRouter now performs stricter validation on listener registration. Renderer processes that attempt to register listeners with invalid parameters (e.g., mismatched extension ID) will now be terminated. (5cca17e)
  • To mitigate a potential vulnerability, a timeout has been added to the Variations Service seed fetch request, preventing an on-path attacker from delaying the response to manipulate the browser's network time. (ad917ff)
  • For Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC), the UnexportableKeyService API was updated for improved type safety. Callbacks now return a role-specific UnexportableSigningKeyId to prevent key misuse at compile time. (402bd74) (4efe919)
  • On macOS, depth limits have been added when processing pasted bookmark data to prevent a stack overflow crash from maliciously crafted pasteboard content. (c5f67dc)
  • The CorsURLLoader now blocks modification of the Origin header during a cross-origin redirect, hardening against potential spoofing attacks. (fd71efb)
  • On Windows, Module Tampering Protection is now enabled for Code Integrity Guard (CIG) processes to defend against IAT hooking by third-party kernel drivers. (60632ed)

Extensions

  • The precedence for which extension handles a given MIME type has been redefined with a new 3-tier rule. This may alter which handler is invoked in environments with multiple extensions handling the same MIME type. (acf2d3c)
  • The installation process for extensions driven by sync or policy has been updated to use the new update_client stack, where it previously defaulted to the old stack for the first install. (e585ba1)
  • Foundational work has been added to the Tabstrip API to support a future context menu. (c46e867)

Browser & Core APIs

  • A new virtual method, OnFederatedLogin, has been added to the WebContentsObserver interface, allowing observers to be notified when a federated login (FedCM) request completes. A default implementation is provided. (17d438b)
  • A new API, performance.speculations.preloads, has been added to expose preload measurement data on the Performance object. (2f2079d)
  • In the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP), the Target.TargetInfo object now exposes a parentId, providing the ID of the parent target (e.g., the frame that created a worker). (96647e3)
  • A new StartupCommandLineCollector class was introduced for enterprise reporting, allowing the collection of command-line switches at browser startup on desktop platforms. (380c663)

Browser UI

  • The Tab Search button in the Horizontal Tab Strip has been reverted to use a Chevron icon. The Magnifying Glass icon is now reserved for the Vertical Tab Strip. (14d4393)
  • In the Reading Mode side panel, nav elements are now hidden and default text decorations for certain semantic HTML tags have been removed to improve the reading experience. (a61f150) (750a791)
  • A fix was implemented to prevent significant overpainting and redundant paint scheduling on the AI page action icon when dragging a browser window. (835b00a)

Networking

  • Support for older, experimental crypto versions for Private State Token (PST) redemption has been removed. Only the stable PrivateStateTokenV1Voprf version is now allowed. (b57cd02)

Build & Testing

  • The required Xcode version for macOS builds has been updated to 17E202 with SDK 25E251. (287de9b) (8e4be44)
  • Modularization efforts continue, with components like notifications, subresource_filter, policy, and local_discovery being moved into their own build targets to improve build times and dependency management. (4ae8b1e) (e514a41) (70f09b8) (1b7e251)
  • The SyncTest::SignIn() helper method was updated to no longer enable history sync by default, better reflecting the default user experience in tests. (b91e14d)
  • The TestPasswordStore class was refactored to remove internal accessors, forcing tests to use the public API for interacting with the store. (a0a2d2f)

2026-04-16

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-16

Overview

This release includes 705 commits, with 539 identified as relevant to developers working on Chromium-based projects. The day was marked by numerous significant breaking changes to core APIs, the extension system, and browser architecture. Key themes include the ongoing modularization of the codebase, modernization of APIs, and major shifts in developer workflows for extension installation. The first relevant commit was (681ab58) and the last was (e9645d9).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Major architectural refactoring has removed the widely-used public utility functions chrome::FindBrowserWithProfile(), chrome::FindLastActiveWithProfile(), and chrome::FindBrowserWithTab(). Embedders must migrate to the new ProfileBrowserCollection and GlobalBrowserCollection classes, which often return a BrowserWindowInterface* instead of a Browser*, requiring significant code updates. (2ac4e0f) (e8d0df4) (adc5dd3) (372e207)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The workflow for installing extensions via the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) has been fundamentally altered. The --enable-unsafe-extension-debugging flag is no longer required, but extensions installed this way are now non-persistent and will be removed on browser restart. This impacts any development or testing automation that relied on the previous behavior. (dddf05b) (0333892)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public interface of components/password_manager/core/browser/LoginDatabase has been changed to use the new StoredCredential struct instead of PasswordForm. Embedders interacting directly with the password database must adopt the new struct. (fa3099d) (167b1b4) (6b01163)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The extension APIs permissions.add and permissions.remove now require a user gesture to show the host access request button in the toolbar. Programmatic triggering of this UI without user interaction is no longer possible, breaking extensions that relied on this behavior. (1aca864)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The legacy IPC ParamTraits serialization for media::AudioParameters and media::ChannelLayout has been removed. Embedders must migrate to the modern Mojo traits for serializing these core media types across process boundaries. (d8a0c10)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public interface content::TracingDelegate has a new pure virtual function, CreateChromeMetadataPacketRecorder(). Embedders with custom TracingDelegate implementations must provide an implementation for this method. (853791d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A new MimeHandlerStreamDelegate interface has been introduced, changing the signature for adding stream containers. Embedders with custom MIME handlers (e.g., for PDF) must adopt the new delegate pattern. (85e6b90)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Core extension management classes ManifestV2ExperimentManager and WebstoreInstallHelper have been relocated from //chrome/browser/extensions to //extensions. This requires updating include paths and build dependencies in projects that use them directly. (c1c2d43) (220dbf0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API WebApp::last_launch_time() now returns a const std::optional<base::Time>& instead of a const base::Time&. Code using this PWA management API must be updated to handle the optional return type. (66b19f5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The CloudPolicyManager and its subclasses have been refactored to replace protected store member variables with getter methods. Custom policy manager implementations that directly accessed these members will be broken. (249fe05)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature for javascript_dialogs::AppModalDialogManager::AppModalViewFactory has been changed to accept a std::unique_ptr<AppModalDialogController>, requiring custom factories to take ownership of the controller. (457861e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public gfx::KeyframeModel class in the compositor stack has been modified, replacing the time_offset_ member with hold_time_. Embedders using this API for custom animations must update their code. (bc70524)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The feature flag kApiPrintingMarginsAndScale has been removed and the feature is now permanently enabled. This alters printing behavior for any application that relied on this flag being disabled. (035abaf)

Extensions & Developer Tools

  • A long-standing memory leak in the declarativeContent API has been fixed. The logic in ExtensionAction::ClearAllValuesForTab was updated to correctly clear declarative state only when a tab is destroyed, preventing unbounded memory growth. (8749e7d)
  • An experiment has been added to toggle the removal of the nacl_arch attribute from the runtime.getPlatformInfo() API, continuing the deprecation of Native Client. (b01d58a)
  • For easier performance analysis, SQL-related trace events will now be grouped under a common parent track in Perfetto (chrome://tracing). (6e2d657)

Security & Privacy

  • A potential sandbox escape vulnerability via drag-and-drop has been fixed. The filename_extension provided by a renderer is now sanitized to remove directory components. (5ef4980)
  • The crypto library now supports P-256 ECPrivateKey structures, adding new methods to create keys from and export to the RFC 5915 format. (6769bc8)
  • Web Bluetooth advertisement filtering has been strengthened by applying the blocklist within requestLEScan to prevent restricted data from being sent to the renderer process. (1f60444)
  • The Private State Tokens (formerly Trust Tokens) feature has been hardened to allow only the V1VOPRF crypto version for issuance, removing support for experimental versions. (591232d)
  • Multiple memory safety bugs were fixed, including a Use-After-Free in PageActionControllerImpl and a dangling pointer in InterceptNavigationThrottle by migrating to WeakPtr usage. (88b9624) (34db318) (dd17b61)

Browser & UI

  • A new method, GetNonDecoratedClientAreaBoundsInScreen, has been added to FrameView to return client area bounds excluding window decorations like shadows, enabling more accurate widget occlusion detection. (cf68817)
  • On macOS, the updater client library (CRURegistration) now includes a checkNowWithCompletionHandler method, allowing applications to programmatically trigger an update check and receive a callback. (b8d9265)
  • Helper functions for creating ui::DataTransferEndpoint and content::ClipboardEndpoint have been centralized into content/public/browser/clipboard_types.h to unify clipboard logic. (32963b6)
  • The DownloadToolbarUIController has been migrated to use UnownedUserData, a modern pattern for attaching data to a Browser instance. (711e8fc)

Networking & Performance

  • To improve performance, the Network Quality Estimator now uses an LRU cache for IsPrivateHost results, reducing synchronous DNS lookups during page loads. This is controlled by the kNetworkQualityEstimatorIsPrivateHostCache feature flag. (fd3f411)
  • On Windows, the initial network connection type check can now be deferred to a background thread at startup to prevent blocking the UI thread, gated by the kDeferConnectionTypeAtStartup feature flag. (fbe4cae)
  • Noisy and unreliable system-wide PDH monitoring on Windows has been removed in favor of more accurate per-process metrics. Several related Memory.Experimental.* and CPU.Experimental.* histograms were obsoleted. (b9c5c38)

Build System & Infrastructure

  • The ongoing modularization effort continues, with components like //chrome/browser/privacy_sandbox, //chrome/browser/share, and //chrome/browser/password_manager being split into separate interface and implementation targets to improve dependency hygiene. (92b2a6c) (3ef188b) (9ddae42)
  • User Education features in Chrome for Testing builds are now controlled by a runtime configuration switch (--chrome-for-testing-confg) instead of being disabled via compile-time #ifdefs, offering more flexibility. (5758f21)

2026-04-15

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-15

Overview

This summary covers 549 relevant commits out of a total of 712 on the main branch for April 15, 2026. The day was marked by an unusually high number of breaking changes, particularly impacting browser lifecycle observation, core networking interfaces, and several extension APIs which have been restricted to ChromeOS. Developers on Windows will see build performance improvements and continued modernization away from legacy ATL libraries. First commit: (b572266)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((b572266)), Last commit: (170c470)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((170c470)).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Browser Lifecycle Observation Removed: The BrowserListObserver class and its notification mechanism have been completely removed. Embedders must find a new way to monitor browser window creation and removal. The related utility function chrome::FindAllTabbedBrowsersWithProfile has also been deleted. (2ebf735) (4650246)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Extension APIs Removed from Desktop: The chrome.usb and chrome.bluetooth* family of APIs have been removed from Windows and macOS builds, restricting them to ChromeOS only. Extensions relying on these APIs for hardware interaction on desktop platforms will no longer function. (1e44322) (32b3c6d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Core Networking Mojo Interfaces Changed: Opaque [Native] types in network_param.mojom (e.g., for HttpResponseHeaders, CertVerifyResult, SSLInfo) have been replaced with fully defined Mojo structures. This is a fundamental change requiring significant updates for any code directly interacting with these network service interfaces. (d825538)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Browser UI API Removals: Several public methods on the BrowserWindow interface related to the bookmark bar (e.g., IsBookmarkBarVisible, FocusBookmarksToolbar) have been removed. Control has moved to the BookmarkBarController. Additionally, PinnedToolbarActions::GetCastButton() and SetActionElementIdentifier() were removed from the toolbar API. (aaed67f) (27ee3df) (9746e16)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING New Pure Virtual Methods Added to Public Interfaces: Embedders implementing content::DevToolsManagerDelegate must now provide an implementation for the new AllowInspectingTarget method. Similarly, implementers of AccessibilityAnnotatorBackend::Observer must add OnContentAnnotationsDeleted and OnContentAnnotationsCleared. (bfdcab0) (8add9cd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public API Signature Changes: The content::URLDataSource::GetReplacements() method was removed, breaking WebUI data sources. The gfx::NativePixmapPlane constructor now uses uint32_t and uint64_t instead of int. The content::IdentityRequestDialogController::ShowModalDialog method added a new callback parameter. (7c2b6cb) (8c46973) (47ad706)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Behavioral Security Change for HLS Media: HTTP Live Streaming (HLS) media can no longer use byte-range requests for cross-origin content that would taint the origin. This security fix may break applications that relied on this behavior for media playback. (d2e04a1)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Behavioral Security Change for Extension Workers: A strict same-origin check is now enforced for DedicatedWorker main script fetches within Extensions and Isolated Web Apps. This may break extensions that were legitimately loading workers from a different origin. (8bde565)

Browser & UI

  • The chrome://policy page now correctly formats JSON values for policy conflicts, improving readability for administrators. (e5f168f)
  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in the desktop FedCM (WebID) UI was fixed by adding WeakPtr checks to prevent access to destroyed UI objects during synchronous callbacks. (60dc402)
  • Accessibility on Windows has been improved by exposing heading levels to screen readers via UIA properties. (74f73f3)
  • A crash when creating a new profile has been fixed by sanitizing the avatar index read from user preferences. (df8537c)
  • The observer list for RenderViewContextMenuBase is now re-entrant, preventing crashes from nested observer notifications during menu construction. (5a97077)

Extensions

  • A new dictionary-based format for the mime_types_handler manifest key is being introduced behind the kApiMimeHandler feature flag. This will allow extensions to handle MIME types like application/pdf without plugins. (50bea86)
  • The Declarative Content API has been hardened against compromised renderers by validating CSS selector matches and ensuring the extension has host permissions before script injection. (3995e72)
  • A security vulnerability in the extension downloader was fixed; it no longer sends authentication headers on redirects to non-Google URLs. (c9de882)

Networking & Security

  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in the File System Access API was patched. This introduces a new RemoveFileChangeObserver method to the SandboxFileSystemBackendDelegate API. (cfafd42)
  • A new enterprise policy, DataUrlInWebWorkerOpaqueOriginEnabled, was added to allow administrators to temporarily revert to legacy origin behavior for data: URL workers. (8d1a1e1)
  • The payment manifest cache key was changed from a hash to the full URL spec to fix a security issue. This will cause a one-time invalidation of existing cache entries. (cf568b2)
  • The net::HashValue::FromString function has been deprecated as part of a move towards a safer factory function pattern. (11fbf92)

Build, Test, & Developer Experience

  • Windows Build Performance: A new GN argument, use_lld_prefetch_inputs, was added for Windows builds to enable the /prefetch-inputs linker flag, significantly improving link times for large targets on cold builds. (8b450df)
  • Windows ATL Removal: A significant refactoring removed dependencies on legacy ATL security classes (e.g., CSecurityDesc) from the Updater and Enterprise Companion components, replacing them with modern equivalents from base::win. (9425684)
  • A new Clang flag, -fdiagnostics-show-inlining-chain, has been added to improve the clarity of compiler warnings and errors by showing the inlining context. (8f58188)
  • A new build flag was introduced to help developers identify performance costs associated with base::span bounds checks. (09ffe4b)
  • A centralized FakeSendTabToSelfModel was created to consolidate multiple test doubles into a single, maintainable fake for unit and browser tests. (4c6f0eb)

2026-04-14

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-14

Overview

This daily summary for April 14, 2026, covers 538 relevant commits out of a total of 707 on the main branch. This period was marked by an unusually high number of breaking changes affecting core browser APIs, networking interfaces, and UI components. Key themes include significant API refactoring to improve safety and modularity, security hardening for the extensions platform, and continued efforts to break up monolithic build targets. The first relevant commit is (841492d) and the last is (59d034a).

APIs & Core Interfaces

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: Widely used browser finder utilities chrome::FindTabbedBrowser and FindAllBrowsersWithProfile() have been removed. Embedders must migrate to using the ProfileBrowserCollection API, which provides similar functionality but with a different access pattern and return types. (c7811f0) (82371d1)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The public method HelpBubble::AddOnCloseCallback() in the User Education framework has been removed. It is replaced by two new methods, AddOnClosingCallback() and AddOnClosedCallback(), to provide more granular control over teardown logic and prevent use-after-free bugs. (e7b9096)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The public method PolicyInvalidator::Shutdown() has been removed. The class and its subclasses now rely on RAII (destructor-based cleanup), simplifying the class hierarchy. Manual calls to Shutdown() must be removed. (2248ccf)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The signature for content::WebContents::SetTabSwitchStartTime has changed to include a new boolean parameter, had_saved_frame_at_start. This change improves tab switch metric accuracy on macOS and requires all call sites to be updated. (424a1a6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The os_crypt component on Windows no longer supports decrypting data encrypted with pre-M79 versions of DPAPI. This permanently removes the fallback for very old profiles and may result in data loss for embedders supporting users who have not opened their profile since late 2019. (db40741)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: Perfetto tracing data source name constants have been moved from the public mojom interface services/tracing/public/mojom/perfetto_service.mojom to a new C++ header. Embedders relying on code generation from this mojom file for these constants will break. (61a9b97)

Networking

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: A major refactoring of core networking Mojo interfaces in network_param.mojom has removed the use of deprecated [Native] types for structures like SSLInfo, CertVerifyResult, and HttpResponseHeaders. Embedders must update their code to use the new, non-opaque Mojo types and handle nullability checks now enforced by the deserializer. (c565345)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The NetworkContext::SetNetworkConditions API, used for network emulation, now requires a throttling_client_id parameter. This allows multiple clients (e.g., DevTools, extensions) to apply throttling rules without interfering with each other. (c9c9dc6)
  • A use-after-free bug in the DNS host resolver was fixed. The issue occurred when an iterator to a set became invalid after an asynchronous operation. The fix now uses WeakPtrs to ensure memory safety. (707cc25)

Browser UI & Features

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The ToolbarButtonProvider interface has been refactored to no longer expose the concrete AppMenuButton. It now returns a new, more abstract AppMenuControl interface. This change is a prerequisite for a future WebUI-based app menu and breaks embedders who directly accessed the concrete button class. (0800caa)
  • A new enterprise policy, ForceForegroundPriorityForOrigins, was added. This allows administrators to specify websites that should run with foreground process priority even when their tabs are in the background. (0593232)
  • The feature flag kOfferPinToTaskbarInfoBar has been removed on Windows, and the feature is now enabled by default. Embedders can no longer use this flag to disable the 'Pin to Taskbar' UI prompt. (b0065ae)
  • A new debugging page, chrome://subresource-filter-internals, has been added. It includes a global checkbox to highlight ads on all pages, simplifying testing and debugging of ad-blocking features. (a4b1f36)
  • The experimental kWebUITabStrip feature has been fully removed from the codebase, finalizing its deprecation. (9a547ba)

Extensions

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The chrome.scripting.executeScript API with allFrames: true will now fail for any subframe where the extension does not have host permissions. This security hardening may break extensions that previously relied on the ability to inject scripts into all frames regardless of permissions. (e05c69e)
  • The extension updater will now use the newer update_client stack for initial extension installations driven by policy or sync, unifying the installation and update logic. (072b716)
  • As part of ongoing modularization, the ExtensionAllowlist and mv2_experiment_stage.h header have been relocated from //chrome to //extensions to reduce dependencies. (925b145) (277ef26)

Graphics, Media & Performance

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The virtual method signature for SkiaOutputSurface::EndPaint in the VIZ display compositor has changed, removing the is_overlay parameter. Embedders who provide their own implementation of this core interface must update their subclasses. (8c12826)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The public constructor for HDRMetadataHelperWin on Windows has been changed. Embedders must now use the new static Create() factory method instead of constructing the class directly, as part of a refactor to remove its dependency on D3D11. (10dba43)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The OnDroppedFrame callback has been removed from the public webrtc::EncodedImageCallback interface. Implementations that override this deprecated method will no longer compile. (92e3162)
  • On Windows, performance counters (PDH) will now be recorded for most Chrome processes to gather more detailed metrics. This is controlled by the kRecordPerformanceCounters feature flag. (ddd1c8e)
  • A bug was fixed on macOS where audio devices could report zero channels, causing errors. The audio manager now validates the channel count before proceeding. (ad60665)

Security

  • A recursion depth limit has been added to bookmark deserialization to prevent a potential stack overflow from a maliciously crafted clipboard or drag-and-drop payload. (0e3f531)
  • The Service Worker script loading process now verifies the main script URL in the browser process. This prevents a compromised renderer from downgrading security policies (like COEP) by forging a request for a different same-origin script. (0f7954e)
  • The cc::RefCountedBuffer class is now thread-safe, inheriting from base::RefCountedThreadSafe to prevent race conditions when the buffer is shared across threads. (5625e13)
  • To protect against 'enterjacking', the Payment Sheet UI will now ignore keyboard events for a brief period after appearing, matching existing protection for mouse clicks. (baccdff)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • A new helper, SyncTest::SignIn(), has been introduced to standardize the sign-in process in sync integration tests, and many existing tests have been migrated to use it. (5138827)
  • The InProcessBrowserTestMixin has been extracted into its own header file to break a problematic transitive include chain and fix compilation for certain test targets. (9e207fd)
  • Several components, including page_load_metrics and media_galleries, were modularized into their own build targets to reduce the size and complexity of the monolithic //chrome/browser target. (0ec6be5) (5b56cb8)

2026-04-13

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-13

Overview

This digest covers a selection of 457 relevant commits out of a total of 613 from the Chromium main branch. The changes span significant build system refactoring, numerous breaking API modifications, and enhancements to security and browser features. The first relevant commit in this period is (129c0a6) and the last is (c2e9a49).

Build System & Core Internals

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: Major build system modularization efforts have moved sources into new, more granular targets. Code related to chrome/browser/net and chrome/browser/enterprise has been extracted from the monolithic //chrome/browser:browser library. Embedders who implicitly depended on these sources will experience build failures and must update their BUILD.gn files to explicitly depend on //chrome/browser/net and //chrome/browser/enterprise. (888790c) (8a22588)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The SubscriptionEligibilityService has been moved from chrome/browser/subscription_eligibility/ to the more generic components/subscription_eligibility/. This refactoring changes include paths and build dependencies for any embedder using this service. (e039a44)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The platform-independent CPU yield method base::subtle::YieldProcessor() has been removed from the public header base/synchronization/lock_subtle.h following a revert. (cd356a8)
  • To improve stability on the Beta channel, several CHECK assertions in core navigation code have been reverted to DCHECK. This will prevent crashes while the root causes of the asserted conditions are investigated. (ab71e92)
  • The Clang and Rust toolchains have been updated to a new version. (e259c09)

Networking & Security

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: A new pure virtual method, ShouldUseFirstPartyCookieContext, has been added to the content::ContentBrowserClient public interface. Embedders who provide their own implementation of this class will have a build break until they implement the new method. (9f52b02)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: Default arguments have been removed from several virtual methods in the core net::CookieStore interface, such as SetCookieWithOptionsAsync. Subclasses and call sites must now explicitly provide all arguments. (4d84d8a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The public function MitigationsEnabledFor3pcd, used for checking third-party cookie deprecation mitigations, has been removed from components/content_settings/core/browser/cookie_settings.h. (1190624)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The utility function MakeInitialResourceRequestWithoutHeadersForPrefetch has been removed from the public content/browser/preloading/prefetch/prefetch_resource_request_utils.h header, making it an internal implementation detail. (535e566)
  • Security on macOS has been hardened by blocking the applescript: scheme from being used as an external protocol, mitigating a potential vector for local attacks. (71c38a1)
  • The enterprise feature EnableEncryptedFileUpload is now enabled by default, enhancing security for file uploads in managed environments. (3e0b8b2)

APIs & Services

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The autofill::mojom::PasswordSuggestionRequest mojom interface has been modified. The uint64 fields username_field_index and password_field_index have been replaced with autofill::FieldRendererId fields username_field_id and password_field_id. (61c155e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The PdfViewerStreamManager class has been renamed to MimeHandlerStreamManager. This is a mechanical but breaking rename affecting any code that interacts with the PDF/MIME-handling stream management system. (c62b1b4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The PrintManagerHost::ShowScriptedPrintPreview() mojom interface signature has changed. The is_modifiable parameter has been removed, requiring updates to any client code calling this method. (d9e628f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The AccountSettingService has been refactored from a concrete class into a pure interface to improve testability. Embedders must update their code to use the new AccountSettingServiceImpl or mock the interface. (8a38787)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) has been updated to add an optional parentFrameId to Target.TargetInfo for worker targets. This change could break clients with strict parsing of CDP messages. (2fce35d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The unused start_suggest_service and search_provider_observer classes and their headers have been removed from components/search/, constituting a public API removal. (66862cd)
  • On Windows, the WebAuthnCreatePinWhenSystemUvDisabled feature is now enabled by default, allowing users to create a PIN for WebAuthn even if system-level user verification (like Windows Hello) is disabled. (d29e714)

Browser UI & Features

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The widely used utility function chrome::FindLastActive() now returns a BrowserWindowInterface* instead of a Browser*. Embedders must update their code to use the new interface, for example by calling GetBrowserForMigrationOnly() on the result to retrieve the old Browser* pointer. (c6268a4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The text autosizer feature, which automatically inflated text sizes on some pages, has been completely removed. This includes the removal of the text_autosizing_enabled and device_scale_adjustment properties from the public WebPreferences API. (4797e6b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The FullscreenController API has been simplified by removing the public method FullscreenTransitionCompleted(). Its logic has been merged into WindowFullscreenStateChanged(), requiring callers to adapt to the new, simpler API. (59472e1)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: Helper functions for finding existing tabs (GetIndexOfExistingTab, GetIndexAndBrowserOfExistingTab) have been removed from the public header chrome/browser/ui/singleton_tabs.h and moved to an internal utility file. (3d5e08b)
  • On macOS, application audio capture can now target applications by their Bundle ID in addition to their PID. This allows for more reliable audio capture from multi-process applications where audio is not handled by the main process. (dce1294)
  • The chrome://policy/logs page has been migrated from the legacy WebUI messaging system to a modern, type-safe Mojo-based interface. (6a07b82)

Media & Graphics

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: An overload of VideoFrame::WrapSharedImage has been removed from media/base/video_frame.h. Callers must now use the version that does not take a coded_size parameter, as this value is now inferred from the shared image itself. (b14f02a)
  • A thread-safety issue in the Media Foundation Video Encode Accelerator (MF VEA) on Windows has been fixed by moving all shared image stub access to the GPU main thread, preventing potential race conditions. (a87beb0)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The deprecated testing utility TestBrowserClosedWaiter has been removed from chrome/browser/ui/test/. Downstream tests relying on this class must be migrated to use the ui_test_utils::BrowserDestroyedObserver class directly. (dd0cca1)
  • A new runtime configuration option, --chrome-for-testing-confg, has been added to Chrome for Testing to allow control over the Search Engine Choice logic in automated test environments. (2553a5f)
  • The deprecated secondary_account_helper test infrastructure has been removed. Callers have been refactored to use the modern SyncServiceImplHarness and SyncTest APIs. (bdfb9c2)

2026-04-12

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-12

Overview

This digest covers 11 commits from (7745160)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((7745160)) to (7752854)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((7752854)). After filtering for changes relevant to browser embedders, 6 commits were analyzed, identifying one major breaking change in the Extensions API.

Extensions

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The virtual method GetPrefServiceForContext has been removed from the public ExtensionsBrowserClient interface. Embedders who provide their own implementation of this class must now use user_prefs::UserPrefs::Get(context) to retrieve the PrefService for a given BrowserContext. This change will break any downstream implementations that were overriding this method. (2a97ba7)

Browser UI

  • The browser UI received several updates, including performance optimizations for vertical tab animations and the addition of zoom bubble support for WebUI-based browsers. A bug was also fixed in the WebUI pinned toolbar to correctly handle non-pinnable actions. (43868b0) (efcc15d) (8768309) (9824296)

Payments

  • Additional logging was introduced for the Facilitated Payments feature to record whether a detected iframe shares the same origin as the main frame. This is an internal metrics change with no external impact. (e3ac2ce)

2026-04-11

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-11

Overview

This digest covers 33 commits from (7748946)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((7748946)) to (7745865)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((7745865)). After filtering for changes relevant to desktop embedders and ignoring internal, platform-specific, and rendering engine updates, this summary highlights 11 key changes across 5 categories, including one major breaking change in the Networking API.

Networking & Performance

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The factory method PrePrefetchService::Create in content/public/browser/pre_prefetch_service.h has been updated to support pre-calculating request headers on the UI thread. The signature changed from Create(BrowserContext*) to Create(BrowserContext*, std::optional<url::Origin> initial_origin_hint, bool initial_javascript_enabled_hint, bool initial_should_append_variations_header_hint). This change, aimed at improving prefetch performance by reducing thread hops, requires embedders to update their calls to this method. (508c306)
  • As a security enhancement, the network stack now blocks forbidden HTTP methods (TRACE, TRACK) for no-cors requests, where they were previously permitted. This aligns Chromium's behavior more closely with security best practices and the CORS specification. (d513cd2)

Security

  • A potential use-after-free (UaF) vulnerability in the Chrome Remoting protocol's authenticator logic has been fixed. The change replaces base::Unretained with base::WeakPtr for callbacks, ensuring that pointers are invalidated if the authenticator object is destroyed. (174583c)
  • A global-buffer-overflow vulnerability in Cast Remoting's protocol handling was addressed. The fix introduces SafeCastAsContiguousEnum to safely cast integer values from incoming protos to their corresponding enum types, preventing out-of-bounds reads when processing pipeline statistics. (0ff4ac1)

Browser UI

  • As part of the "Glow Up" project, new alternate colors for tab groups have been introduced, along with a feature flag to control them. The changes are reflected in the tab strip, the tab group editor, and the WebUI tab search menu. (87013f2) (a69fa3e) (3efb98d)
  • The "Learn More" link in the PWA installation dialog has been updated to use a persistent support.google.com p-link, ensuring the link remains valid even if the underlying help article URL changes. (081cba1)
  • On Windows, the TSFEventRouter has been refactored to use the Windows Runtime C++ Template Library (WRL) instead of the legacy Active Template Library (ATL) for COM object implementation. This is part of a larger effort to modernize the codebase and remove ATL dependencies. (cc9e302)

Extensions

  • The build configuration for chrome/browser/speech has been updated to use the ENABLE_EXTENSIONS_CORE flag. This change helps align extension-related speech API behavior on desktop Android with other desktop platforms. (ae8e697)

Base Library

  • The base::AutoReset utility class has been updated to use raw_ptr<T> instead of a raw C++ pointer for its internal variable. This is a memory safety improvement that leverages MiraclePtr's protection without altering the public API of the class. (c8d4183)

2026-04-10

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-10

Overview

This summary covers 595 commits on the main branch, with 447 identified as relevant to developers building on Chromium. The changes span from the first commit (52f8bb1) to the last (8f5b39c). This digest highlights numerous breaking API changes, security enhancements, and significant updates to core browser components and UI.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API for creating GPU contexts, such as viz::ContextProviderCommandBuffer::CreateForRaster, has been changed. The lose_context_when_out_of_memory parameter was removed, and embedders must update their calls. (b7eadee)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The net::URLRequestJob public interface has been updated. Methods like GetTotalReceivedBytes now return the type-safe base::ByteSize instead of int64_t. Embedders with custom protocol handlers must update their method signatures. (72bf5dc)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The searchbox.mojom interface was refactored to use a factory pattern. Consumers must now bind a searchbox::mojom::PageHandlerFactory instead of directly binding the PageHandler, breaking custom WebUI implementations. (a43ea83)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ModelBrokerClient API in Optimization Guide now uses string-based use_case identifiers instead of a mojom::OnDeviceFeature enum. Embedders using on-device models must update their code. (dfe0d80)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The base::task::sequence_manager::ThreadControllerImpl class and its factory function CreateSequenceManagerOnCurrentThread have been removed. Embedders must migrate to CreateSequenceManagerOnCurrentThreadWithPump. (36e7905)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The recently added content::CdmHelper public interface and its header content/public/browser/cdm_helper.h have been reverted and removed completely. (7b96ee6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public constructor for media::EncryptionPattern is now private and has been replaced by a static Create() factory method that returns an std::optional. Direct instantiation will no longer compile. (006ccbd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The legacy ManagePasswordsIconView class and its header have been removed as part of a UI migration. Code referencing these components will fail to build. (822db52)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the updater's PolicyService, the public function AreUpdatesSuppressedNow has been renamed to AreUpdatesSuppressed and its signature was changed to require an explicit base::Time parameter. (d166a73)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The AccessibilityAnnotatorBackend::Observer public interface was modified. The OnContentAnnotationsAdded method now requires a const ContentAnnotationsData& parameter, breaking existing observer implementations. (fd9d5b4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The FetchEmailOneTimeTokenResponse protobuf message in the One-Time Token Service API has been changed, removing the created_timestamp and received_timestamp fields. (4ce640a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The legacy GlicFreUI has been deleted, along with its public headers such as glic_fre_ui.h. Any code referencing this UI will no longer compile. (dc4535b)

Browser & Core APIs

  • The BrowserContext::UniqueId() method is now deprecated. A new BrowserContext::UniqueToken() method returning a more secure base::UnguessableToken should be used instead. (4f81ffc) (f2112b5)
  • Asynchronous methods have been added to PageContentExtractionService, allowing callers to wait for pending page analysis results. (691d55a)
  • A new UnexportableSigningKeyId type was introduced to provide stronger type safety for hardware-backed signing keys. (a2bcfb9)
  • On Windows, a DCHECK failure in VideoCaptureDeviceFactoryWin was fixed by preventing unnecessary restarts of the COM thread, improving stability. (2edfa92)
  • The WebCrypto API now supports ML-KEM-768 and ML-KEM-1024 for post-quantum key encapsulation. (b582009)

Security

  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in WebContentsImpl::ForSecurityDropFullscreen was fixed by using WeakPtr to safely handle WebContents instances during nested message loops. (899e77f)
  • A re-entrancy bug in TabStripModel that could cause a Use-After-Free during tab move operations has been fixed. (0985a47)
  • For FedCM, a check was added to validate the initiator's origin when processing Set-Login headers in the browser process, enhancing security for subresource loads. (5932bfd)
  • The XKB keyboard layout name parser was hardened to prevent potential path traversal attacks. (73daddb)
  • Integer overflow vulnerabilities in the H.264 parser were fixed by using checked arithmetic. (4986289)
  • A Use-After-Free in the DevTools agent host was patched by preventing double initialization. (5b7fd82)

UI & Browser Features

  • The "Send Tab to Self" feature will now display a toast notification on desktop platforms when a tab is automatically opened. (eecf3ec)
  • The updater UI on Windows now supports high DPI scaling, resulting in a sharper interface. (c154701)
  • The "Default Search Engine Reset" dialog has been refactored to use ui::DialogModel, updating its text and converting the "Learn more" button into an inline link. (6f48ee3)
  • A dangling pointer crash in the bookmark context menu on macOS was resolved by converting a raw_ptr to a WeakPtr. (c31e869)
  • The Task Manager was updated to correctly display prerendered new tabs by adding a new PrerenderNewTabTag. (7c4cbb7)
  • A new enterprise policy has been added to allow overriding the CPU Performance API. (1bff823)

Extensions

  • A crash in the OneTimeMessageHandler has been fixed. The handler now validates that the ScriptContext is still active before processing a message response, preventing a use-after-free. (94a8ea5)
  • A crash in ExtensionSyncService was fixed by adding a null check when retrieving granted permissions, preventing issues with incomplete sync data. (0eeafe7)
  • Content Security Policy (CSP) enforcement was hardened for sandboxed extension service workers. (a0b8451)

Networking

  • The WebSocket implementation over HTTP/3 was enhanced by implementing GetLoadTimingInfo, GetSSLInfo, and GetRemoteEndpoint in the handshake stream. (9a70b09)

Build & Infrastructure

  • The enable_surface_embed build flag has been removed, making the feature's code always available when WebContents is used and simplifying build configurations. (b27035e)
  • On Windows, usage of the Active Template Library (ATL) has been removed from content_main.cc as part of a larger effort to remove ATL from the codebase. (fc1e63c)
  • Numerous components continue to be modularized to improve build structure and dependency hygiene, including push_messaging, nearby_sharing, and headless browser mode. (da19370) (37803b9) (b4167ed)

2026-04-09

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-09

Overview

This daily summary for April 9, 2026 covers 528 relevant commits out of a total of 721 on the main branch. A significant number of breaking changes were landed, primarily focused on refactoring core browser APIs, removing legacy functionality, and cleaning up data structures. The first relevant commit was (f3cfa32) and the last was (1b28d58).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The widely used public functions chrome::FindBrowserWithWindow and chrome::FindBrowserWithID have been removed. Embedders must update their code to use the new GlobalBrowserCollection singleton, for example: GlobalBrowserCollection::GetInstance()->FindBrowserWithWindow(). The return type for the window lookup has also changed from Browser* to BrowserWindowInterface*, requiring further refactoring. (2a4a86d) (fb39bae)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The integer values of the public media::Channels enum in media/base/channel_layout.h have been changed to align with the WAVEFORMATEXTENSIBLE standard. Any code that relies on the specific numeric values of these channels for audio processing will break. (898ad6b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A new pure virtual method, NotifySwappedRWHVChildFrameFromRenderManager, has been added to the content::RenderFrameHostManager::Delegate interface. Custom implementations of this delegate must now provide an implementation for this method to avoid build failures. (b83f5c5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the base threading library, Thread::Delegate::AddTaskObserver and the corresponding task_observer option have been removed. It is no longer possible to add a TaskObserver upon thread creation. (e501d62)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ocsp_result field has been removed from the public net::SSLInfo struct as part of the deprecation of the Expect-Staple feature. Code inspecting OCSP verification results via this field will no longer compile. (f366471)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The FontService::ListFamilies() method has been removed from the public font_service.mojom interface. Clients using this IPC to enumerate system fonts will be broken. (bb555c3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Windows, the public methods Emf::InitFromFile() and Emf::InitToFileForTesting() have been removed from the exported printing::Emf class. (2abeb2f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The rule_options member has been removed from the content_settings::RuleMetaData struct and its corresponding mojom definition, breaking any code that reads or writes to it. (e8b22be)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public operator== has been removed from the passage_embeddings::Embedding and history_embeddings::UrlData data structures, which will break tests or other code relying on direct equality comparison. (1aa8ca6)

API & Core Infrastructure

  • The TabGroup class in components/tabs now has an observer pattern, allowing clients to subscribe directly to group and visual data changes, simplifying UI updates. (9236242) (e74b83d)
  • As part of a large-scale refactoring, many components like ChromeTracingDelegate and SmartRestartMetrics were migrated away from the legacy BrowserList to use the new GlobalBrowserCollection and its observer. (6f905a1) (a9834f7) (59169c4)
  • Several components were updated from the legacy MemoryPressureListener to the new MemoryConsumer API for improved memory management. (5bf4747)
  • On Windows, dependencies on the legacy ATL framework were removed from UI automation, accessibility event waiters, and the updater in favor of modern WRL and base library equivalents. (c434495) (f3cfa32)
  • A new BrowserLaunchEvent proto was added to the enterprise reporting pipeline to capture browser launch information. (c6c9731)

Security & Privacy

  • To prevent a compromised renderer from spoofing audio activity, the browser process now verifies that a renderer is authorized to bypass the browser's audio service before marking a tab as 'audible'. (c1f3553)
  • A same-origin check is now enforced for main script fetches in Dedicated Workers to prevent a compromised renderer from exfiltrating assets from other origins. This is controlled by the kEnforceDedicatedWorkerSameOriginCheck feature flag. (7d3c950)
  • On Windows, a potential local privilege escalation vulnerability in Chrome Remote Desktop was fixed by using user impersonation when writing WebAuthn extension files. (98ca974)
  • For enterprise users, deep scanning for downloads now supports user-initiated cancellation. (d858c06)
  • The experimental ChallengeURL prototype was removed from the WebAuthn API as it was not on a standards track. (24b759e)

UI & Features

  • The WebUI home button now supports drag-and-drop for setting the homepage URL. (5bc04ab)
  • An implementation was added to automatically open tabs received via the Send-Tab-To-Self feature on desktop platforms, controlled by a new feature flag. (a77fda5)
  • On macOS, an issue causing the titlebar to get stuck in fullscreen when using vertical tabs has been resolved. (72c5f64)
  • Accessibility for the password generation popup has been improved for screen reader users by ensuring the popup widget is activated, allowing for proper navigation. (939b475)
  • The Windows updater UI has been polished with a smoother, anti-aliased progress bar and adjusted button colors. (64c765f) (79e3c49)

Testing & Tooling

  • Chrome for Testing (CfT) now supports a runtime configuration JSON file, specified by the --chrome-for-testing-config switch. The initial implementation adds an option to enable a virtual clipboard. (bca0df9)
  • The eventToPromise() test utility in WebUI has been updated to be strongly typed with TypeScript, improving test code quality and maintainability. (adadafb) (49febf0)
  • The Extreme Lightweight UMA Detector (ELUD) now embeds object and allocation sizes into its zap pattern, aiding in the diagnosis of memory corruption bugs from crash reports. (cbcea52)

2026-04-08

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-08

Overview

This summary covers 793 commits on the main branch, with 622 identified as relevant to developers working on Chromium-based projects. The changes span from commit (fea075a) to (1572e92). This was an exceptionally active day for API refactoring, resulting in a large number of breaking changes across core utilities, browser interfaces, and networking. Other notable changes include ongoing security hardening and UI refinements to context menus and side panels.

Breaking Changes: Core APIs & Interfaces

  • ⚠️ BREAKING base::ObserverList now disallows re-entrant iteration by default on desktop platforms. The default policy has been changed from kAllowReentrancy to kDisallowReentrancy. Code that relies on nested observer notifications will now trigger DCHECK failures and must be updated to explicitly use the kAllowReentrancy policy. (83dc1d0) (cf7e006)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The BrowserWindow public interface has been significantly refactored. The pure virtual method ZoomChangedForActiveTab() has been removed, and numerous utility functions in signin, sync, and web_applications now accept a BrowserWindowInterface* instead of a Browser*. Custom BrowserWindow implementations must be updated. (0501de8) (c7fb45c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The method for querying browser instances has been changed. The free functions chrome::GetTotalBrowserCount() and chrome::GetBrowserCount() have been removed. Callers must migrate to the new GlobalBrowserCollection and ProfileBrowserCollection APIs. (ebcb57e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The extensions::RuntimeAPIDelegate::OpenOptionsPage method is now asynchronous. Its signature has changed from returning a bool to returning void and taking a base::OnceCallback<void(bool)>. Embedders with custom implementations must update to the new signature. (011e693)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The content::RenderWidgetHostView public interface has a new pure virtual method, HasSavedFrame(). Embedders with custom implementations of this interface must add an implementation for this new method to avoid compilation errors. (1e42bd2)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public virtual method ContentBrowserClient::IsIsolatedContextAllowedForUrl has been removed. Embedders must now implement the new DirectSocketsDelegate::AreDirectSocketsAllowed to grant access to APIs like Direct Sockets. (5697236)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public constructor for AuthenticatorWin now requires a gfx::NativeWindow. Code that relied on the previous constructor, which automatically found the active browser window, must be updated to explicitly provide the native window handle. (3df2ead)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The RecordReplayDriver class has been refactored into a pure abstract interface. Embedders directly using the old concrete class must update their code to use the new ContentRecordReplayDriver implementation and associated factory patterns. (560f67a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The static method TranslateLanguageList::DisableUpdate() has been removed. Tests that used this method to prevent server-side language list updates must be updated to use the new constructor that allows injecting a mock TranslateUrlFetcher. (6aef37c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Multiple base and content APIs have been changed. This includes replacing std::map with base::FieldTrialParams in feature list APIs, removing GetIgnoredDuplicateNavigationCount() from content::NavigationHandle, and refactoring base::trait_helpers to use base::ParameterPack. (9aa846e) (2950468) (0b91f88)

Breaking Changes: Networking & Mojo

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Significant Third-Party Cookie Deprecation (3PCD) logic has been removed from the network stack. This includes the removal of the kTpcdMetadataStageControl and kTpcdMetadataGrants features, TPCD_METADATA and TPCD_HEURISTICS content setting types, and related DevTools parameters. Embedders relying on these for 3PCD integration will need to adapt. (708fe41)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING APIs for secure cookie exemptions have been migrated from scheme/host-based to origin-based. This changes method signatures in content_settings::CookieSettings and net::CookieAccessDelegate, requiring embedders to update how they configure cookie exemptions. (1575beb)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ContentSettingsManager::AllowStorageAccess mojo interface signature has been changed. The origin, site_for_cookies, and top_frame_origin parameters were removed, as this information is now derived from the RenderFrameHost on the browser side. (8c04824)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The chrome.mojom.PluginInfoHost.GetPluginInfo() Mojo interface has been changed. The origin input parameter was removed to prevent the renderer from supplying an incorrect origin. Consumers and implementers of this interface must update to the new signature. (3c9de1b)

Security

  • Multiple security vulnerabilities were addressed, including a Use-After-Free in PasswordFormManager, a state leak in the File System Access API, a UAF in WebSocketSpdyStreamAdapter, and a potential out-of-bounds write in the GPU command buffer. (c542194) (a4c6a71) (5d8f582) (dc5e20c)
  • WebAuthn "Immediate Mode" with passwords and passkeys has been enabled by default. (7b93b74)
  • A potential browser crash during printing has been fixed. The browser now safely rejects an invalid memory handle from the renderer instead of crashing. (7d72d49)

APIs & Extensions

  • The views::WebView class now includes a SetOwnedWebContents() method, allowing it to take ownership of an externally created WebContents instance. (a203cc3)
  • The PageContentExtractionService has new asynchronous methods (Get...Async) that wait for initial content extraction to complete, preventing race conditions on page load. (9bd9cbe)
  • Service worker registration for extensions will now be retried on a transient kErrorNotFound error, improving reliability in certain enterprise environments. (0c3687a)
  • The network::DataElement public API was enhanced with a TryAs<T> helper to simplify type checking and data access in a single operation. (09fd4dc)

Browser UI & Features

  • The "Send to your devices" option in context menus has been converted into a dynamic submenu that directly lists available target devices, controlled by the kSendTabToSelfShowTargetsInContextMenus feature. (1399633)
  • The "Tabs From Other Devices" side panel now has an entry point in the 3-dot menu and its availability is now restricted to regular profiles (excluding incognito and guest). (0964b4e) (33653e7)
  • On macOS, support for the AXTextOperation parameterized attribute was implemented, enabling bulk text operations like find-and-replace for accessibility clients. (dea0b52)
  • The TabStrip WebUI playground at chrome://tab-strip/, an early testing ground for the feature, has been removed as it is now obsolete. (5e4e689)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • ChromeDriver was optimized to avoid redundant focus script calls if an element is already active, which could previously cause issues with contenteditable elements. (a310a5c)
  • A large-scale refactoring migrated numerous keyed service factories from the Singleton pattern to the preferred NoDestructor pattern for improved memory management and shutdown behavior. (6d036b4)

2026-04-07

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-07

Overview

On April 7, 2026, the Chromium main branch saw 582 commits, with 502 identified as relevant to developers. This day was marked by a large number of significant breaking changes, primarily driven by the 'Project Bedrock' refactoring which replaces Browser* pointers with a new BrowserWindowInterface. Other major breaks include the removal of third-party cookie deprecation APIs, changes to core networking interfaces, and an upgrade of the TypeScript compiler. The first commit of the day was (e1a3d4f) and the last was (5a63e24).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The 'Project Bedrock' refactoring continues, replacing raw Browser* pointers with the narrower BrowserWindowInterface* in many public APIs. This affects core utilities like chrome::FindBrowserWithTab, session service helpers in session_service_lookup.h, and UI coordinators like CastDialogCoordinator. Embedders must update call sites to handle the new interface. (78da6a2) (d9ac938) (05f77bc)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public getters on BrowserWindowFeatures for accessing UI controllers like LensOverlayEntryPointController and TabSearchToolbarButtonController have been removed. Access must now be done via the static ::From(browser) method on the respective controller classes. (6d74b29) (a89d948)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ui::views::BubbleAnchor type has been refactored from a variant alias into a dedicated class. This changes the public API for views::BubbleDialogDelegateView and other components that create UI bubbles, requiring significant updates to bubble creation code. (1ba6a69)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Core networking APIs have changed. Methods in net::HttpTransaction that return byte counts now use the net::ByteSize class instead of int64_t. Additionally, the Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) API on net::URLRequest was changed from set_allows_device_bound_sessions(bool) to set_device_bound_session_mode(net::DeviceBoundSessionMode). (79bbb97) (b057ca4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING As part of the ongoing third-party cookie deprecation (3PCD) cleanup, the TpcdMetadataManager and its factory have been deleted entirely. Furthermore, the public method net::CookieSettingsBase::IsExemptFromThirdPartyCookieBlocking and related exemption reasons have been removed. (b7f05fa) (c9934fd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING For security hardening, the extension downloader will no longer follow HTTP redirects when fetching CRX files. This is a major behavioral change that will break custom or enterprise extension update servers that rely on redirects. (04fad69)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The TypeScript compiler has been upgraded to v6.0.2, which deprecates older syntax and may require updates to custom WebUI code. The public testing utility PrerenderTestHelper::AddPrerenderUntilScriptAsync also had its signature changed. (83a142e) (e3d4fd8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several other public APIs were changed or removed: sql::Database::ReportMemoryUsage was deleted, the AutofillSuggestionDelegate interface was modified, the kBackgroundMediaCapture feature flag was moved out of the public content::features namespace, and the DataTypeStore::DeleteAllDataAndMetadata signature was updated. (b93f6d1) (5fe250d) (3ecbb63) (bbdfaaf)

Security

  • A Use-After-Free (UAF) vulnerability in ServiceWorkerCacheStorageMatcher was fixed by improving the lifetime management of the script sender. (d17fe11)
  • The Viz service now validates TextureQuad data during deserialization to harden the browser against malicious renderer processes. (acafd83)
  • A path traversal vulnerability on Windows was fixed in ChildProcessSecurityPolicyImpl to better handle parent directory references like ... (8f31946)
  • A one-minute grace period was added for Signed Certificate Timestamp (SCT) validity checking to better tolerate minor clock skew between clients and servers. (f7d2cdc)

Networking

  • The public net::ResolutionDetails struct was expanded to include task_completion_delay and secure_dns_attempted, providing more detailed information about DNS resolutions. (e3c1d6a)
  • A performance issue causing inefficient one-byte reads in HttpStreamParser has been fixed. (4168e93)
  • A bug was fixed in the Connection Allowlist feature to ensure violation reports are sent to the correct endpoint specified by the document's Reporting-Endpoint header. (5564461)

Browser UI & Features

  • The ui::menus::SimpleMenuModel API has been enhanced to support secondary labels, allowing for richer context menus. (a6fb10a)
  • A new context menu item has been added to allow pinning and unpinning the tab search button in the tab strip. (04d2971)
  • An entry point to show and pin the 'Tabs From Other Devices' side panel has been added to the 'Customize Chrome' side panel. (1f82a70)
  • The SyncShowBookmarksLimitExceededError feature, which displays an error when the bookmark storage limit is reached, has been enabled by default. (454badd)
  • The experimental TabStripExperimentService has been removed, with its functionality for updating tab group visuals consolidated into the main TabStripService. (792acce)

Core, Build & Testing

  • A new configuration system was added for Chrome for Testing (CfT), allowing runtime options to be specified via a JSON file with the --chrome-for-testing-config switch. (f908610)
  • A brief but significant breaking change to the allocator_shim::UncheckedAlignedAlloc function signature was introduced and quickly reverted due to build failures. (2b3ee80) (f747855)
  • On Windows, a new feature flag (RemoveGpuJobMemoryLimit) was added to optionally remove the job memory limit for the GPU process, aiding in memory usage measurement for features like WebGPU. (705baec)
  • Several components, including //chrome/browser/feed, //chrome/browser/performance_manager, and //chrome/browser/component_updater, were modularized into their own BUILD.gn files to improve dependency management. (bfec926) (879e523) (c44b7b1)

2026-04-06

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-06

Overview

On April 6th, 2026, 215 of 282 total commits were identified as relevant for developers building on Chromium for desktop platforms. This summary covers changes from commit (95b6d18) to (0c355e9), highlighting two significant breaking changes in the compositor and accessibility APIs, the introduction of a new TabStrip API, and notable updates to networking and security.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public cc::LayerTreeHostClient interface has been changed. The virtual method WillBeginImplCommit was removed, and its logic merged into WillCommit. Embedders with custom LayerTreeHostClient implementations must remove the WillBeginImplCommit override to avoid build failures. The cc::TextureLayer::Create factory method signature was also updated. (7010b87)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: A stability check has been promoted from a debug-only DCHECK to a production CHECK on Windows to prevent re-entrant calls to the public BrowserAccessibilityManager::OnAccessibilityEvents method. Embedders whose code triggers this condition will now experience a production crash, hardening the API but requiring potential code updates. (c52ae89)

Browser & UI

  • A new TabStrip API is being introduced to allow programmatic updates to the tab strip. Initial commits add the service definition and an Update method, starting with support for updating a TabGroup's visual data. (ea91004) (4a588dd)
  • A race condition causing a dangling pointer in BackgroundModeManager was fixed. The fix ensures old status icon menus are kept alive until the context menu update is fully complete, preventing crashes. (620a047)

Networking & Security

  • Detailed DNS resolution information (e.g., source of result like cache, DoH, or system resolver) is now propagated through the network stack. This data is available in LoadTimingInternalInfo and can be used for more granular performance analysis. (2e629dc) (3c5e3ba) (dd8d63c) (7288496)
  • A new feature flag, kIgnoreCorsPreflightPolicy, was added to the Network Service. When enabled, this flag will ignore the kPreventPreflight policy, moving towards its deprecation. (55b0c31)
  • A security fix prevents a potential user activation bypass. System-triggered media suspensions, such as audio focus loss, no longer grant a transient user activation token to the frame. (55823cd)
  • The experimental ServiceWorkerAutoPreload feature for subresources has been removed. This includes the removal of the kAutoPreload enum value from the public blink.mojom.ServiceWorkerFetchHandlerBypassOption interface. (2bb2c50)
  • Bug fixes for Service Workers were landed, addressing a potential use-after-free in ServiceWorkerVersion and a bug that caused requests to stall when a fetch event was restarted. (405dcf0) (bc87e02)
  • The list of bulk HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security) preloaded entries has been updated. (baeca77)

Extensions

  • Several internal enterprise extension APIs, including enterprise.platformKeys and enterprise.networkingAttributes, were migrated from JSON schema to WebIDL as part of an ongoing refactoring. This is not expected to impact extension behavior. (19c7e7f) (f610734) (3440252)
  • The EnterpriseExtensionDOMActivityTelemetry feature, which collects DOM activity for enterprise-installed extensions, has been enabled by default. Its runtime state is now tracked via a synthetic field trial for performance analysis. (1775f9a) (00f7844)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • A new InteractionTestUtilSimulatorWebUI was implemented to allow automated tests to interact with tracked WebUI elements using the same APIs as for Views and WebContents, improving testability of WebUI components. (3609c24)
  • As part of the ongoing modularization of //chrome/browser, source files related to downloads were moved into a new //chrome/browser/download component, breaking a dependency cycle. (38b45dc) (5adc86a)
  • A large-scale code health effort fixed "Include What You Use" (IWYU) violations related to base/logging.h across many files, improving build hygiene. (3be1f60)

2026-04-05

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-05

Overview

This digest covers 3 commits. After filtering for changes relevant to Windows and macOS desktop embedders, 2 commits were analyzed. The changes focus on stability improvements in window management and bug fixes within the payments component. No major breaking changes were identified.

UI & Window Management

  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in TransientWindowManager has been resolved by replacing base::AutoReset with base::WeakAutoReset. This change prevents memory access after the window manager has been destroyed during synchronous callbacks (e.g., window_->Hide()), improving overall browser stability for desktop platforms. (ab2ec9c)

Payments

  • The PaymentLinkManager logic was updated to prevent its state from being modified while a payment link operation is already in progress. This avoids potential race conditions and ensures more predictable behavior for facilitated payments. (2071ecb)
Page 1 of 2
